recentHighlights: By Inferno's Light (S5E15) — The Dominion War's second punch: Cardassia joins the Dominion, Dukat disowns Ziyal, Garak conquers his claustrophobia in a crawlspace to finish Tain's work. The Changeling Bashir nearly destroys the Bajoran sun. The false-invasion trap — warp signatures that don't correspond to real ships. Martok re-born, alliances rebuilt. Written by Behr & Wolfe, directed by Landau.

In Purgatory's Shadow (S5E14) — Garak and Worf discover Internment Camp 371: General Martok imprisoned for two years (every Klingon decision since S3 guided by a Changeling), Doctor Bashir replaced for a month. Tain dies giving Garak the only gift that matters: a childhood memory of a hand held. The wormhole bursts open. "To be continued." Written by Wolfe & Behr, directed by Beaumont.

For the Cause (S5E13) — Eddington's betrayal revealed: he was the Maquis mole the whole time. "Nobody leaves paradise." Kasidy Yates arrested for smuggling to the Maquis. Garak chooses trust — tossing away his phaser to lie beside Ziyal. Sisko swears a personal vendetta: "If it takes me the rest of my life." Written by Ronald D. Moore, directed by Conway.

The Begotten (S5E12) — Two births, two losses: Kira delivers the O'Brien baby, Odo nurtures a dying baby changeling. Mora reconciles with Odo across a lifetime of trauma. The baby changeling merges into Odo's hands, restoring his shapeshifting. Joy and grief in the same heartbeat. "Please, don't die. There's so much I want to show you." Written by Rene Echevarria, directed by Trevino.

For the Uniform (S5E13) — Sisko threatens to poison a Maquis world — and does it. The bluff is indistinguishable from the real thing. Eddington surrenders not because he's trapped but because the script demands it: Valjean must sacrifice himself for Javert. "You didn't clear it with Starfleet, did you?" Written by Peter Allan Fields, directed by Lobl.
